channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Esther Passaris Agrees to Lunch Meeting with Young Man Who Approached Her
item.description: Nairobi women representative, Hon. Esther Passaris, recently responded to a young man, Kennedy Arnold, who approached her publicly with a placard asking to take her out for dinner. Kennedy, who works ...

item.title: Japan Says North Korea May Have Fired Missile While U.S.-Iran-Israel Conflict Continues
item.description: Japan government don talk say dem suspect North Korea don launch one ballistic missile on Saturday. The matter come show for the official X page of Japan Prime Minister Office, wey confirm say securit...

item.title: Van accident in Alluri Sitharama Raju district leaves 20 injured, 3 critical
item.description: On the night of March 13, 2026, a van carrying around 20 workers met with an accident in Ananthagiri mandal, Alluri Sitharama Raju district, Andhra Pradesh. The workers, hailing from Jami in Vizianaga...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard appreciates public backing for WPS transparency efforts
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard has expressed gratitude for the public's support regarding its ongoing transparency initiative related to the West Philippine Sea (WPS). This move highlights the agency's co...

item.title: Alex Eala Faces Key Points Defense at 2026 Miami Open to Protect Top 30 WTA Ranking
item.description: Filipina tennis star Alexandra Eala is set to compete in the 2026 Miami Open, where she earned a first-round bye as the No. 32 seed. She is projected to reach a career-high No. 28 in the WTA rankings ...

item.title: Government to Support Households Facing Increased Heating Oil Prices
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves has announced plans to provide financial support for households struggling with rising heating oil costs, a situation exacerbated by global tensions following the US-Israel co...

item.title: Women Deserve Respect and Recognition Without Conditions
item.description: In this strong opinion piece, Tanzanian journalist Beatrice Materu calls out the unfair expectations and contradictions placed on women in modern society. She questions why women are still judged for ...

item.title: Chancellor could gain over £1 billion in VAT as petrol and diesel prices rise
item.description: Analysis indicates that Chancellor Rachel Reeves may collect an additional £1.1 billion annually in VAT due to the recent surge in petrol and diesel prices, largely influenced by the ongoing conflict ...

item.title: Cubana Chief Priest Admits Error in 2023 Election, Pledges Support for Tinubu
item.description: Cubana Chief Priest, popular socialite and businessman, don come out clear say e regret not to support President Bola Ahmed Tinubu during the 2023 general election. For one political gathering wey hap...

item.title: Allahabad High Court Rules State Cannot Limit Namaz Attendance Without Proper Law Enforcement
item.description: The Allahabad High Court has made it clear that the state has a duty to ensure that every religious community is able to conduct prayers peacefully at a recognised place of worship or on private prope...

item.title: Kenya Nominates Justice Njoki Ndung'u for ICC Judge Position
item.description: President William Ruto has put forward Supreme Court Judge Njoki Ndung'u as Kenya's official candidate for the International Criminal Court (ICC) judicial elections happening in December 2026 in The H...

item.title: Pakistan Ministry Refutes Afghan Taliban Claims of Capturing Military Outpost
item.description: On Saturday, Pakistan’s Ministry of Information and Broadcasting strongly denied claims made by the Afghan Taliban about capturing a Pakistani military post. The Afghan Ministry of Defence had circula...

item.title: Meta Considers Major Job Cuts to Balance Rising AI Investment Costs
item.description: Meta is reportedly considering significant layoffs that could affect over 20 per cent of its workforce as it deals with rising costs from artificial intelligence infrastructure. Sources familiar with ...

item.title: Plateau Pastor Claims US Troops Deployment Skipped Christian Areas, Blames NSA Ribadu
item.description: Rev. Ezekiel Dachomo, wey be Plateau State cleric and also the Barkin Ladi Regional Chairman for Church of Christ in Nations, don come out to accuse National Security Adviser Nuhu Ribadu say e dey div...

item.title: Tanzania and Uganda strengthen cooperation on SGR, trade, and energy projects
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania and Uganda have officially signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to enhance cooperation in several important areas. The MoU focuses mainly on the development of the Isaka...

item.title: Persons deprived of liberty participate in Antipolo’s 2nd district special elections
item.description: Inmates, or persons deprived of liberty (PDLs), in Antipolo City were given the chance to vote on Saturday, March 14, during the special elections for the city’s second congressional district. Accordi...

item.title: South Africa and Eswatini Formalise Revised Komati Basin Water Agreement
item.description: South Africa and Eswatini are set to formally sign a revised treaty on the development and management of the Komati Basin's water resources at Maguga Dam in Eswatini on 13 March 2026. The signing invo...

item.title: Mexico explores teen social media restrictions inspired by Australian model
item.description: Mexico is exploring the possibility of introducing restrictions on social media use for teenagers, taking inspiration from Australia’s recent policy. The Mexican Public Education Secretary, Mario Delg...

item.title: Six Terrorists Killed in Intelligence-Based CTD Operation in Lakki Marwat
item.description: In Lakki Marwat, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, an intelligence-led operation conducted by the Counter-Terrorism Department (CTD) resulted in the deaths of six terrorists. The incident occurred in the Shagayi ar...

item.title: Grace Tame on activism, public backlash, and standing for human rights
item.description: In this exclusive piece for Crikey, Grace Tame reflects on the political and social backlash she faced after being labelled 'difficult' by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and the controversy that foll...
